title: Dacryodes barteri
intro: Dacryodes barteri, also known as African walnut, is a deciduous tree species native to West and Central Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dacryodes barteri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Dacryodes barteri include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments, particularly skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Dacryodes barteri is classified within the family Burseraceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the torchwood or frankincense family. This family is part of the order Sapindales, which is a large order of flowering plants. The order Sapindales is further classified within the clade Rosidae, which is a clade of flowering plants that also includes the order Malpighiales and others. Dacryodes barteri is a species of flowering plant and is native to tropical regions of Africa. The species is often found in forests and woodlands, where it grows as a tree.
uses: Dacryodes barteri is a plant species with reported medicinal uses, including treating fever, rheumatism, and other health conditions.
